steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what function the underlined sentence serves. It explains why horizontal gene transfer matters: it can increase bacterial antibiotic resistance, and understanding how to prevent it could help mitigate dangerous pathogens.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It suggests that a commonly held belief regarding an organism is mistaken.: The sentence does not correct a mistaken belief; it identifies a practical reason to study horizontal gene transfer. ✗
  • Choice B — It summarizes the history of research into a biological phenomenon.: The sentence does not summarize research history; it focuses on a possible practical benefit of understanding how to prevent horizontal gene transfer. ✗
  • Choice C — It illustrates why one organism may pose more of a threat than another.: The sentence does not compare one organism with another; it explains that horizontal gene transfer can increase bacterial antibiotic resistance. ✗
  • Choice D — It explains a practical benefit of researching a particular biological process.: The sentence explains a practical benefit of researching horizontal gene transfer: preventing it could help mitigate dangerous pathogens. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
